text:
Once upon a time in a small forest village lived a girl named Justina. Justina loved animals. Every evening after school she'd head deep into the forest to look for frogs or small birds. But one day she stumbled upon something entirely different.

In the moonlight she saw an owl perched on a branch. It was unlike any owl she'd ever seen. Instead of feathers brown-striped feathers it had turquoise! Justina drew closer. That's when she noticed the big red eyes. Was that a talking fox? Perhaps just half-wolf.

The owl hooted softly. Justina hopped closer.

"Hello," she said.

The owl cocked its head.

"All my life I've heard stories. Just listen." He took flight, gliding slowly.

Justina learned a lot in his quiet way. The fox liked tales of bravery. She told him of the time she saved her rabbit buddies from a hawk. The owl clapped his wings heartily.
